records.ety: [OE. liven, livien, AS. libban, lifian; akin to OS. libbian, D. leven, G. leben, OHG. lebēn, Dan. leve, Sw. lefva, Icel. lifa to live, to be left, to remain, Goth. liban to live; akin to E. leave to forsake, and life, Gr. liparei^n to persist, liparo`s oily, shining, sleek, li`pos fat, lard, Skr. lip to anoint, smear; -- the first sense prob. was, to cleave to, stick to; hence, to remain, stay; and hence, to live.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Lived (lvd); p. pr. & vb. n. Living.]

defs: To be alive; to have life; to have, as an animal or a plant, the capacity of assimilating matter as food, and to be dependent on such assimilation for a continuance of existence; as, animals and plants that live to a great age are long in reaching maturity.
quotes: Thus saith the Lord God unto these bones; Behold, I will . . . lay sinews upon you, and will bring up flesh upon you, and cover you with skin, and put breath in you, and ye shall live.

defs: To pass one's time; to pass life or time in a certain manner, as to habits, conduct, or circumstances; as, to live in ease or affluence; to live happily or usefully.
quotes: O death, how bitter is the remembrance of thee to a man that liveth at rest in his possessions!

defs: To make one's abiding place or home; to abide; to dwell; to reside; as, to live in a cottage by the sea.
quotes: Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years.

defs: To be or continue in existence; to exist; to remain; to be permanent; to last; -- said of inanimate objects, ideas, etc.
quotes: Men's evil manners live in brass; their virtues We write in water.

defs: To enjoy or make the most of life; to be in a state of happiness; as, people want not just to exist, but to live.
quotes: What greater curse could envious fortune give Than just to die when I began to live?

defs: To feed; to subsist; to be nourished or supported; -- with on; as, horses live on grass and grain.
To have a spiritual existence; to be quickened, nourished, and actuated by divine influence or faith.
quotes: The just shall live by faith.

defs: To be maintained in life; to acquire a livelihood; to subsist; -- with on or by; as, to live on spoils.
To outlast danger; to float; -- said of a ship, boat, etc.; as, no ship could live in such a storm.
quotes: A strong mast that lived upon the sea.

defs: To spend, as one's life; to pass; to maintain; to continue in, constantly or habitually; as, to live an idle or a useful life.
To act habitually in conformity with; to practice.
Having life; alive; living; not dead.
quotes: If one man's ox hurt another's, that he die; then they shall sell the live ox, and divide the money of it.

defs: Being in a state of ignition; burning; having active properties; as, a live coal; live embers.
Full of earnestness; active; wide awake; glowing; as, a live man, or orator.
Vivid; bright.
Imparting power; having motion; as, the live spindle of a lathe; live steam.
Connected to a voltage source; as, a live wire.
Being transmitted instantaneously, as events occur, in contrast to recorded.
Still in active play; -- of a ball being used in a game; as, a live ball.
Pertaining to an entertainment event which was performed (and possibly recorded) in front of an audience; contrasted to performances recorded in a studio without an audience.
Life.
